name: 南京
description: Nanjing, ancient capital of six dynasties and capital of the Republic of China, is a city of profound historical significance. Home to the Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum, Ming Xiaoling Tomb (UNESCO), and theMemorial Hall of the Victims in Nanjing Massacre. A cultural crossroads between north and south China.
highlights: Ming Xiaoling Tomb - UNESCO World Heritage

climate.tips: Best in spring for flowers or autumn for foliage. Summers hot and humid. Winters cold with occasional snow. Spring plum blossoms and fall gingko are spectacular.

attractions.name: 中山陵
attractions.description: Magnificent mausoleum honoring Dr. Sun Yat-sen, founder of the Republic of China, on Purple Mountain with 392 steps.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds. Wear comfortable shoes. Combine with Ming Xiaoling Tomb nearby.

attractions.name: 明孝陵
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage site - the tomb of Emperor Hongwu, founder of the Ming Dynasty, with spectacular Avenue of Stone Animals.
attractions.ticketPrice: 70 CNY (includes Ming Xiaoling and Music Square)
attractions.tips: Walk the 600-year-old avenue at sunrise for magical light. Best in autumn for golden gingko. Combine with nearby Purple Mountain.

attractions.name: 南京大屠杀纪念馆
attractions.nameEn: Memorial Hall of the Victims in Nanjing Massacre
attractions.description: A powerful memorial to the 300,000 victims of the 1937 Nanjing Massacre, with exhibits and pilgrimage hall.
attractions.tips: Bring tissues. Audio guide available. Allow time for reflection. Combine with nearby Fuzi Temple.

attractions.name: 总统府
attractions.description: The presidential office of Sun Yat-sen and later Chiang Kai-shek, now a museum showcasing 600 years of history.
attractions.tips: Good audio guide. Large complex with much history. Morning visits recommended.

attractions.name: 夫子庙
attractions.description: Ancient temple complex with traditional architecture, markets, and boat rides on the Qinhuai River.
attractions.tips: Best at night when illuminated. Try local snacks from vendors. Take boat ride on river.

attractions.name: 紫金山
attractions.description: A 450-meter mountain south of the Ming Xiaoling with hiking trails, ancient temples, and panoramic city views.
attractions.tips: Plan full day to cover major sites. Take cable car up, walk down. Combine multiple attractions.

attractions.name: 玄武湖
attractions.description: A beautiful urban lake with parks, gardens, and the ancient Ming city wall views, perfect for cycling and walking.
attractions.tips: Rent bikes around the lake. Combine with nearby Purple Mountain. Beautiful at sunset.

attractions.name: 老门东
attractions.description: Restored historic district near Fuzi Temple with traditional architecture, crafts, and local snacks.
attractions.tips: Best evening with lighting. Try duck blood soup and small sesame balls. Less crowded than Fuzi Temple area.

restaurants.name: 南京大牌档
restaurants.address: Multiple locations including Xuanwu and Fuzi Temple
restaurants.description: Popular chain serving authentic Nanjing and Jiangsu cuisine in traditional atmosphere.

restaurants.name: 狮子桥
restaurants.description: One-diamond Black Pearl restaurant known for refined Jiangsu cuisine and elegant dining.

restaurants.name: 尹氏鸡汁汤包
restaurants.description: Legendary tiny soup dumplings in rich chicken broth, a Nanjing institution since 1920s.

restaurants.name: 回味鸭血粉丝汤
restaurants.description: Popular chain famous for duck blood noodle soup, a quintessential Nanjing breakfast.

restaurants.name: 咸亨酒店
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant inspired by Lu Xun's famous work, serving Shaoxing wine and traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: 绿柳居
restaurants.description: Famous vegetarian restaurant established 1912, known for meat substitutes and Buddhist cuisine.

restaurants.name: 小笼包店
restaurants.description: Local breakfast spots serving Nanjing-style soup dumplings and congee.

restaurants.name: 秦淮人家
restaurants.description: Riverside restaurant serving quality Nanjing cuisine with evening boat ride option.

arrival.tips: Airport 40km from city center. Airport bus to city (5 CNY) or taxi (150 CNY). Metro LineS1 to downtown.

arrival.to: Nanjing Railway Station / Nanjing South Station
arrival.tips: Nanjing South for high-speed from Shanghai/Hangzhou. Nanjing Station for Beijing/Shanghai slow trains. Both have metro.

metro: Connects to both stations and airport
bike: Unlock: 1 CNY, then 0.5-1 CNY per 30 min

hotels.name: 南京金陵饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Book well ahead. The original 5-star in Nanjing. Sunday brunch is famous.

hotels.name: 南京香格里拉大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for first-time visitors. Book river view rooms.

hotels.name: 南京绿地洲际酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Newer property with best views. Great for business travelers.

hotels.name: 状元楼酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for sightseeing. Book during off-peak season.

hotels.name: 南京国际青年旅舍
hotels.bookingTips: Great for backpackers. English speaking staff.

culturalTips.title: Memorial Culture
culturalTips.content: Nanjing is deeply connected to Chinese modern history. Visit the Massacre Memorial Hall for reflection. The Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum represents founding of modern China. Presidential Palace shows ROC era.

culturalTips.title: Nanjing Cuisine
culturalTips.content: Nanjing food is savory and slightly oily. Must-try: duck blood soup, salt duck, soup dumplings. The city's cuisine reflects its position as 'the home of Jinling cuisine'.

culturalTips.title: Avoiding Crowds
culturalTips.content: Nanjing's attractions can be very crowded. Visit Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum and Ming Xiaoling early morning. Fuzi Temple is best at night. Weekdays are quieter.

culturalTips.title: Seasonal Highlights
culturalTips.content: Nanjing's gingko trees are legendary. The turn color in mid-November along银杏大道. Cherry blossoms at Xuanwu Lake in spring. Plum blossoms in winter.

emergencyContacts.name: 美国驻上海总领事馆
emergencyContacts.notes: For US citizens in Nanjing region

quickFacts.water: Tap water safe after boiling; bottled water recommended
